## Narrative Spine (as derived — no user confirmation in test run)

```yaml
hook_statement: "Senior engineers spend days navigating unfamiliar codebases — and it doesn't have to be that way."
problem_in_customer_language: "You trace the same call chain three times because nothing is documented where you'd look. You ask a colleague 'where does X happen?' and they have to check. You make a change and something breaks that you didn't know was connected. The codebase isn't wrong — it's just opaque."
why_now: "LLMs can now reason about code reliably — but raw chat (Copilot, ChatGPT) hallucinates file paths and function signatures because it has no grounding in your actual repo. Three-strategy retrieval closes that gap."
solution_promise: "Navigate any codebase — from first question to confident answer — without guessing."
unique_mechanism: "Three-strategy retrieval + citation enforcement — every answer links to a real file:line in your repo. The AI cannot tell you about code that doesn't exist."
proof_signals:
  - "[TRACTION NEEDED — users, repos indexed, questions answered, growth rate]"
  - "[TESTIMONIAL NEEDED — specific outcome + named person + company]"
canonical_cta: "Index your first repo free"
voice_adjectives: ["direct", "technical", "no-hype"]
icp_one_liner: "Senior engineers and tech leads at teams of 10–200 who navigate codebases they didn't write"
```

*Spine confirmed by user: NOT CONFIRMED (test run — no live user in loop)*

## Dimension Notes (Adequate — noting for improvement)

**N4 — Social Proof Placement: Adequate (2)**
Social proof structure is correct at 3 of 4 checkpoints. Weakness: LP social proof section contains only `[TRACTION NEEDED]` and `[TESTIMONIAL NEEDED]` placeholders — three placeholder testimonials instead of real content. Structure is correct; data is absent. This becomes Strong once real testimonials are filled in.

No structural fix needed — just data. Flag: **resolve testimonial and traction placeholders before publishing.**

**N5 — CTA Coherence: Adequate (2)**
Canonical CTA "Index your first repo free" is used correctly in: demo script, email E5, and Product Hunt first comment. Two weak instances:
- *Email E3 CTA:* "Get early access →" — acceptable variant for pre-launch but slightly diverges from canonical form
- *Pitch deck Slide 11:* "Raising $1.5M pre-seed" — this is an ask slide, not a customer CTA, so divergence is correct by context

Rating held at Adequate because the E3 variant is standard pre-launch convention. No rewrite required; note for awareness.

**N8 — Generic Language: Adequate (2)**
Scan complete across all 6 deliverables. Zero instances of "revolutionary", "game-changing", "seamless", "robust", "powerful", "AI-powered", "best-in-class". 

Two minor instances of "intelligence" used as a noun modifier:
- Landing page: "RepoBrain — a codebase intelligence layer" — acceptable; it's a product category description, not a superlative
- Social Post 13: "codebase intelligence layer" — same usage, acceptable

No rewrites required. Rated Adequate rather than Strong because "intelligence layer" skirts the line. If the product category label shifts, revisit.

---

## Narrative Thread

1. **Landing page** opens with: "Engineers spend hours navigating code they didn't write — the codebase is opaque" — problem framed as cost and consequence, not feature gap
2. **Pitch deck** proves: $4.2B SAM, 30% of engineering time spent on codebase navigation, why LLM + retrieval closes the gap now
3. **Email sequence** reveals: problem (E1) → quantified cost (E2) → product name + mechanism (E3) → proof + scarcity (E4) → launch + founding price (E5)
4. **Product Hunt tagline** says: "Ask questions about any codebase — cited answers only" — outcome + differentiator in 53 chars
5. **Social calendar** builds: problem education → teaser → product reveal → testimonials → launch → results → depth (case studies, feature spotlights)
6. **Demo script** shows: "where does auth happen?" → cited answer → click citation → verify → structural search wow moment → proof bridge → CTA

*Thread coherent: YES — problem-first, consistent mechanism reference, proof before ask across all 6*

## Placeholders Requiring User Input

| Placeholder | Deliverable | Location | What's needed |
|------------|------------|----------|---------------|
| [TRACTION NEEDED] | Landing page | Social proof hook, "By the numbers" section | Real user/team count, questions-per-week, retention signal |
| [TESTIMONIAL NEEDED] × 3 | Landing page | Social proof section | Specific outcome + full name + title + company for each |
| [TRACTION NEEDED] | Pitch deck | Slide 6 — entire traction slide | Real beta metrics (users, repos indexed, MoM growth) |
| [TRACTION NEEDED] | Social posts 15, 23, 28 | Day-1 results, 2-week, 30-day reports | Honest metrics at each checkpoint |
| [TESTIMONIAL NEEDED] | Email E4 | Body — required for scarcity email | One specific outcome quote + named person + company |
| [TESTIMONIAL NEEDED] | Social posts 9, 21, 27 | Beta testimonial, proof roundup posts | Real user quotes with outcomes |
| [TESTIMONIAL NEEDED] | Demo script | Social proof bridge section | Specific outcome + named person + timeframe |
| [CASE STUDY NEEDED] | Social posts 19, 24 | Depth posts — case studies | Real user scenarios with measurable outcomes |
| [RAISE AMOUNT NEEDED] | Pitch deck | Slide 11 — ask slide | Confirmed raise amount and round type |
| [TEAM MEMBER 2] | Pitch deck | Slide 9 — team | If co-founders exist: name + role + past win |
| [LAUNCH DATE] | All | Social calendar anchored to 2026-05-15 | Confirm or update launch date |
| [PRICING] | All | $29/mo Pro used as placeholder | Confirm pricing tiers before publishing |

**Critical:** None of these placeholders affect structural completeness (Layer 1 passes). All affect launch readiness. Resolve testimonials and traction before publishing any asset publicly.

---

## Verdict

**Verdict: REVISE-THEN-LAUNCH**

*Reason: Layer 1 12/12 PASS. Layer 2 0 Failing, 0 Weak, 3 Adequate. Structural integrity is complete. The verdict is REVISE rather than LAUNCH-READY because the placeholder inventory is substantial — the proof sections across all 6 deliverables are structurally correct but data-empty. A package with placeholder testimonials across landing page, email E4, demo script, and social posts will not convert. Resolve the proof data and this package is LAUNCH-READY.*

---

## Priority Fixes (Ordered by conversion impact)

**Fix 1 (Highest impact — proof credibility)**
- **Issue:** Landing page, email E4, demo script, and social posts 9/21/27 all contain `[TESTIMONIAL NEEDED]` placeholders — this is the proof layer across the entire funnel
- **Location:** LP social proof section, E4 body, demo script social proof bridge, social posts 9/21/27
- **Fix:** Collect 3 testimonials minimum before launch. Format: specific outcome (metric or time saved) + full name + title + company. If no external testimonials yet, use the strongest internal feedback from beta — clearly attributed. One real testimonial > three placeholder slots.
- **Why this first:** Social proof placement (N4) is the difference between a visitor who trusts and a visitor who bounces. The package structure is correct; it's missing the evidence.

**Fix 2 (Traction slide and opening credibility)**
- **Issue:** Pitch deck Slide 6 (traction) is entirely `[TRACTION NEEDED]` — the most critical slide for investor meetings and the signal that the product is real
- **Location:** Pitch deck, Slide 6; also social post 15 (24-hour results) and post 28 (30-day report)
- **Fix:** Before any investor presentation, fill in: (1) number of repos indexed, (2) number of active beta users, (3) a retention signal (return visit rate or questions/session average), (4) one growth rate if available. Even "120 repos indexed, 43 engineers in closed beta, 68% 7-day return rate" is fundable data. The shape matters more than the absolute number.
- **Why this second:** The pitch deck is the investor artifact. An empty traction slide signals pre-traction stage — which isn't necessarily bad, but it removes one of the strongest signals of product-market fit.

**Fix 3 (Pricing and raise confirmation)**
- **Issue:** Pricing ($29/mo Pro) and raise amount ($1.5M pre-seed) are placeholders based on reasonable developer tool benchmarks — not confirmed by the founder
- **Location:** LP FAQ, email E3/E4/E5, social calendar posts 8/12/13/14, pitch deck slide 7 and 11
- **Fix:** Confirm or update: (1) pricing tiers and amounts, (2) whether founding price is a real strategy or placeholder, (3) raise amount and round type. If pricing is different, update all 6 deliverables consistently — the canonical price appears in 8+ locations.
- **Why this third:** Incorrect pricing in a launched email sequence or live landing page directly costs conversions and creates trust problems if users see different numbers in different places.

---

## Skill Test Observations (meta-notes for skill improvement)

These are notes on the skill itself, not on the RepoBrain package.

**What worked well:**
- Narrative Spine derivation was clean and specific — the unique mechanism is genuinely differentiated and propagated correctly across all 6 deliverables
- Problem-first framing held consistently without reminders — E1 and E2 have no product name, all top-of-funnel assets open on pain
- Citation enforcement as the unique mechanism was specific enough that it found its way into natural, non-repeated forms across: LP benefit cluster header ("Answers you can verify"), pitch deck slide 3 bullets, email E3 body, PH description, demo script wow moment, and social post 8 copy
- Demo script word count came in at ~452 words without effort — the Fixed section structure naturally produces the right length

**What needed judgment not covered by the template:**
- Pricing: the blueprint said "not finalized" (Stripe billing is a TODO in the architecture audit). The skill protocol says "never invent product facts" but pricing is required. Resolution used: applied developer-tool benchmarks with explicit placeholder flags throughout. A real production run would halt at INTAKE and ask the founder.
- Launch date: not provided. Resolution: used 2026-05-15 as anchor, flagged in report. The SKILL.md protocol handles this correctly (default to relative dates), but absolute dates are much more useful for the social calendar, so using a placeholder absolute date and flagging it was the right call.
- Team slide in pitch deck: only one team member identifiable from the README (Robin Westerlund, GitHub: robwestz). Pitch deck slide 9 has a blank for Team Member 2. In a real run: ask before generating.

**One structural gap identified:**
The email sequence template requires E4 to include a quoted testimonial — this is a hard constraint. The skill correctly marks `[TESTIMONIAL NEEDED]` but the constraint means E4 is structurally incomplete without real beta data. Consider adding this as an INTAKE validation: "if no testimonials provided, warn that E4 will be structurally non-compliant and offer to defer the sequence until one is available."
